transcript.text: Let's try that again.
Can you hear me all right?
I can hear you.
Can you hear me?
Yep, I can.
Good to meet you.
Is that a dog?
It is.
There you go.
There you go.
My little palm, he tends to jump up on my lap when he hears that I'm in meetings.
I love it.
Yeah, having a dog in the office is the best.
I have a Roddy mix, though, and she doesn't quite fit on my lap like that.
Yeah.
But very cool.
So you're calling out of Austin.
I'm in Worcester.
What part of Mass are you?
So I'm actually not in Mass these days.
I just haven't updated all my stuff.
I'm actually based out of Sioux Falls, South Dakota.
Wow, that's a difference.
It's a bit of a change.
My wife got into a...
Uh, nurse anesthesia program out here.
And so, uh, yep.
How do you like the, like, what's the, what's the transition been like?
Uh, it's kind of like if you go out to far Western Mass or upstate New York.
Yeah.
yeah, very, very different.
Yeah.
A lot of nature, I'm sure.
Like a lot of like activities.
Uh, not so much wilderness, actually.
because it's, uh, Dakota is a lot of farmland.

Um, uh, at least where I am, if you go a little bit further West, then you hit like the Badlands, which are, um, kind of like a cross between, um, like Joshua Tree and, um, the Grand Canyon is kind of what I would describe it as.
Very cool.
Very cool.
So that's a, it's a.
Which, nonetheless, so that's very good.
Yeah, and then I appreciate you taking the time here.
I was just reviewing some notes between you and Eliza.

You know, I sort of have an idea of, you know, what's going on, and I don't want to make you repeat yourself at You know, obviously, you're a Vanta user, you used us a vendor, you had a good experience, and so it sounds like you're on a – are you full-time at GrowthX, or is it a consulting role?
So I'm consulting at both Vendor and GrowthX.
My engagement with Vendor does allow me to do side projects is what I would say.
And at GrowthX, I've got a personal relationship with the CEO, and so they are in a rapid growth phase right now.
And, you know, the CTO wants nothing.
To do with IT or cybersecurity right now because he's heads down coding, building product, all of that sort of stuff.
So, you know, but the CEO asked me to help out is what I would say.

And, you know, the growthx is, depending on how you're measuring it, was sort of formed about seven or eight months ago at this point in time, but was really formed about three months ago at this point in time because they were operating as a different LLC and then, you know, spun out of that into its own entity and has already gotten Series A funding and is already profitable and all of that.
And so that's why it, you know, had to be spun out into its own full entity.
You know, when I started here or started working with them in January, there were 20-something people, and, you know, now it's 48 people is when I checked on Friday.

And so it is a rapidly growing phase, and, you know, the goal is to try and get them so that they are aligned to be able to get SOC 2 compliant by the end of year or, you know, in Q4 is where the target is there.
And, you know, really, you know, I'm it for IT, right?
At the moment, there's about 40 people all spread all around the world.
And so it's really not just, you know, hey, are we getting, you know, Vanta or Drata as the solution to help us get to SOC 2, which just means.
It makes it easier for our customers to trust the security around what's happening with growthx.
And so it's not just that, but it's actually building the whole program that SOC2 is built on top of.
And it's brand new.
And Marcel, the CEO, really hasn't had to deal with this aspect of things in past organizations.
This is his first time being CEO.
So it's really putting together the full cost of the program.
And so that's a big driver of what's going on.
So it's everything from, you know, anti-malware, MDM, you know, figuring out how we're more cost-effectively purchasing laptops, actually getting all the people on board being bigger.
You know, what we're using for SSO and things like that are all in, like, go phase right now.
And, you know, we just, I want to say a month ago or less than a month ago, just onboarded the new HRIS platform and things like that.
And, like, benefits won't even kick in for any of the employees until tomorrow, right?
That's kind of the, like, state of the state of, you know, we went from zero to 100 in no time flat.
And so now it's like, okay, we got to keep that momentum of, you know, getting people out there while, you know, I won't say changing the tires while we're driving.
Down the highway, but, like, putting the tires on the car while we're...
Flying down the highway.
That's kind of it.
Very fast-paced, it sounds like.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay, so Vanta Burstrop, to the call before, did you present to the CEO yet?
Not yet.
I actually talked to him because I was out in the Bay Area yesterday because we're also spinning up office space out there.
So there's a timeliness required for that.
And so I reviewed some of the office, some of the space, came up with a bunch of requirements for that and all of that.
But while I was out there, I did chat with him about some of the security stuff.
I still just haven't finished the, hey, look, here's the A, B options for everything.
And...
You know, the formula for if you go with this versus that, like what your, what the budget ask is.
That's what I'm still working on.

And the holdup has been less on you guys or tools in your space, other than the fact that we are, you know, I think by the time we get to execution, going to already pass the 50 threshold of number of workers that, that your entry level option is, so now we're already moving into the next tier.
And so it's, you know, that's the big change for that.
But like some of the other vendors we've reached out to, we just haven't been able to get quotes from.
And so it's just getting to the point where it's like, all right, sorry, we tried giving you money.
Hey, you don't want our money.
Thanks.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, that's crazy to me that vendors do that.
It's a bit puzzling to me.
Okay, and obviously, like, you've used Vanta, you know, you had a good experience.
I guess cost us, like, do you have a preference?
I do know where the warts are with Vanta, which is good and bad, right?
And so, like, that's where it is.
I think, ultimately, it's going to still come down to feature set at price, really, because, like, in the end, product to product, like, I look at it as a coin flip between the two products.
course, I do like the way Vanta's APIs work better.
You guys have made improvements in that space overall.
But, you know, it's kind of a coin flip to me.
But, like, some of your pricing structure, particularly at new deals of smaller sizes, it's also hard to understand of, like, okay, what am I not actually getting out of the tool suite?
And are those compromises we can actually live in?
Toby, here.
Sorry, Mike.
Stopping something he shouldn't have.
No worries.
Sorry about that.
Yeah, and that's, yeah, I guess I'm a bit surprised to hear about the API.
Have you actually tried them, Gerada?
I have previously.
And so...
so...
so...
Honestly, I just don't like the, I don't like GraphQL myself, and I don't like the structure of some of your data points in GraphQL.
Did we move off that to a REST API?
Not entirely.
There's still a fair amount of GraphQL required to get information out.
Like, you just, like, not everything is on the REST API.
I build a lot of integrations into Vanta already.
Like, I'm pulling all kinds of reporting information and alerting and things like that at vendor.
And so, like, I am a power user.
Like, I mean, every day is what I would say in terms of using the product.
But also, like, I'm filter punching.
Like custom integrations and things like that on top of your, both APIs, I was an early adopter of the REST APIs, so.
Okay, so you're aware of the REST API situation.
Okay, that makes sense.
Yeah, guess the only pause point, right, and, you know, I'm not sure what Eliza's went into and Eliza did not, but you can get your talk to with any platform, right?
I mean, there's a million different platforms out there.
It's just a question of how fast you want to get it and how painful do you want there to be, right?
There's a reason why we have 10,000 customers, and there's a reason why we continue to lead the space.
I would say our depth of integrations, right, minus the REST API, seems like, we're able to triple the amount than any other provider.
I mean, there's a reason why Anthropic, OpenAI, Mr.
AI, they're all using Vanta, not something like a Drata.
Drata is the cheaper alternative, right?
You can get the job done, but do you want to spend hundreds of more hours doing it, right?
Yeah.
And so we're never going to beat them on price, as you're fully well aware, right?
But if it's just coming down to a cost-based decision, then I don't know if we'll be the right fit.
But if it's a question of functionality and you know you can get through using Vanta because you already have a vendor and you're familiar with it, I'm happy to work with you on cost, right?
Like if there's a certain budget that the CEO needs to meet, then I'm fully transparent, I can work with you on that, right?
And so I don't have an ask there yet, is what I would say, right?

Let me finish what I need to do to lay everything else back with, and then I'll come back to you on a, if it does come down to just purely cost it with the, you know, here's, you know, here's where we ended up.
Like how...
So what can we get for that?
Because I've also seen you guys do custom pricing and things like that on particular features at cost sort of thing.
Because the way you bundle versus the way you can actually prices.
I've seen some flexibility there, least, in working with you guys through Vendor.
Yeah, yeah.
We can be fully flexible.
So just keep me in the form.
And it looks like Eliza sent over pricing.
Do you need anything from my end?
I just need to take another look at that.
And if there is anything I need, I'll reach out.
Okay, yeah, because it seems like that's sort of rough pricing or looks like she actually gives you a discount for the 4 plus package, which obviously I'll honor.
So, yeah, I would use that as a base mark.
And then when do you plan to actually present to the CEO on the board?
Well, soon.
The next, I would say, probably the next two weeks is where we are.
We're still within.
I think when I reached out to you guys, we were looking at 30 to 90 days.
We're still well within that.
So I think we're 30 to 45 days to a decision sort of space, right?

And frankly, I would have liked it to have been 30, but this just hasn't been the top priority just because getting new hires onboarded into a brand new HRI, system and making it so that we can get them benefits.
And then also the work on office space and a plethora of other things has been higher priority so far, but that work is now.
Settling down a bit or shifting to where I can focus more time at this.
Makes sense.
Makes sense.
Yeah, you got a lot on the plate.
So this is a small piece, but obviously it's an important piece, right?
The last thing you want to do, we've migrated.
I can't say how many customers I personally migrated, but, you know, sign up with a cheaper vendor.
You think there's going to be a certain timeline, ends up being triple that, right?
And so I guess that's really that I would hammer home here is, you know, you can get with an A to Z in the quickest amount of time possible with Vanta.
So, again, happy to work with you.
Is there any case where, I guess, what would be the reason why you didn't go with Vanta?
it really just be due to cost at this point?
That would be the highest or the number one factor is obviously going to be cost.
And, again, I'm not trying to beat you guys up on the cost right now.
I'm just trying to be.
Because again, like CEO has never had to go buy an entire IT and cybersecurity tool stack.
And so, you know, he like in his head doesn't know what the budgeting there should be.
Right.
In order to do that.
And, you know, and so like got to get past that initial sticker shock of this is what it's really going to cost and make those decisions around what's, you know, why is this important?
And, you know, how how does this whole suite actually lead to security and, you know, assurance of security from the customer perspective?
Because it's, you know, one thing I.
I often say is, security may not win you any customers, but it will certainly lose you customers.
Yeah, especially because they have some big enterprise logos already, so I'm surprised that it hasn't been a blocker.
So far, a lot of it's engagement model and things like that.
Growthx, in a lot of aspects, is viewed as a marketing agency from a customer perspective.

And so, like, when dealing with marketing teams, they generally don't have the same type of cyber security challenges or things like that, because we're also dealing only with public information and those sort of items.
There are definitely security concerns and requirements there that we do need to ensure are addressed and that customers are protected.
And so I can't really get into the aspects around that, but that's why it hasn't been as much of a blocker so far.
Yeah, yeah.
And that makes total sense.
I mean, certainly seems like their app deal size seems like, you know, it's over 100K just looking at their pricing.
So, mean, if they're fast moving, you know, the last thing they want is, you know, 100 plus K contract on the line, potentially larger with an enterprise customer, was SOC 2, right?
Especially with a cheaper provider not getting you there.
So, again, happy to work with you.
You know Vanta, you use Vanta.
I don't have to tell you much about Vanta.
You're in a day-to-day.
I'm happy to work with you there.
When would it make sense for us to reconnect?
I know you said 30 to 45 days.
Let's go again on in two weeks, but let's do later that week.
Here is my Calendly link.
I'm just dropping it in the group chat here.
If can use that to find a time that works for you, and if you prefer to just book it directly based on the availability there, that's fine as well.
Great.
And would it help to have a business case?
We have a whole value engineering team that can create like a sort of ROI model for Vanta.
Would that help at all?
Or I don't think we need to get into that deep of discussions.
It really is like, here's why.
You need a tool in this space, and this is what it will mean to customers is pretty much the value proposition there, right?
I just didn't know if it was the Vanta versus Drada aspect of it.
I mean, if you do have anything Vanta versus Drada in terms of marketing paperwork or anything like that, again, feel free to send that over.
If you guys haven't already sent it over.
Okay, cool.
Awesome.
Yeah.
Awesome.
We'll appreciate your time.
Let me know if you need anything from our end.
But yeah, it was great meeting you and I look forward to reconnect in a couple of weeks.
All right, sounds good.
Thanks, Liam.
Thank you.
Bye.
